description_long: This rock is an irregularly shaped, medium-sized boulder with a rugged, knobby surface composed of various rounded stones embedded in a lighter matrix. Its coloration is mottled, featuring shades of gray, off-white, tan, and subtle hints of reddish-brown, suggesting a conglomerate composition. The surface is highly textured, with protruding pebbles and uneven indentations, giving it a coarse and natural appearance. The rock appears solid and dense, with no visible cavities or openings, and its form is asymmetrical, with one side more upright and the other more squat and broad. The coloration and weathering patterns indicate exposure to the elements, likely from an outdoor environment. This type of rock could serve as a natural barrier or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A rugged, multi-colored conglomerate rock with a highly textured, knobby surface and irregular, asymmetrical shape. Its mottled gray, tan, and reddish hues suggest natural weathering and outdoor placement.
description_view_0: Tallest side with a prominent upright face, showing a mix of gray and reddish tones and a rough, uneven surface.
description_view_1: Broad, squat side with a rounded profile and a dense cluster of embedded stones, mostly gray and off-white.
description_view_2: Narrower edge with deep indentations and pronounced surface texture, dominated by tan and brownish hues.
description_view_3: Widest, flattest side with a golden-tan coloration and a bumpy, pebble-studded surface.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped public space
